PANW.title: Marvell Technology, Palo Alto Networks, Gamestop, Ulta Beauty And Gitlab: Why These 5 Stocks Are On Investors' Radars Today

macro_events.event_summary: Secretary of State Rubio says Iran has mined large segments of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening ~20% of global seaborne oil flows.
primary_drivers: Crude oil supply shock risk premium

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated with significant upstream leverage to Brent; minimal Strait of Hormuz transit exposure vs Middle East NOCs, so a Brent spike flows directly to upstream earnings.
Same upstream Brent leverage as XOM; Permian and GoM production benefits from crude price spike without Hormuz transit risk.
Pure-play Permian E&P with high oil cut and US onshore production — direct beneficiary of WTI/Brent spike with no Middle East operational risk.
Frontline VLCC tanker operator — Hormuz mining forces rerouting and surging war-risk insurance, driving spot tanker rates sharply higher. Classic Hormuz-disruption trade.
International Seaways crude tanker fleet benefits from ton-mile expansion as Gulf crude reroutes; spot rates historically spike on Hormuz incidents.
Cheniere — ~20% of global LNG transits Hormuz (Qatar). Disruption diverts buyers to US Gulf Coast LNG; spot LNG prices and US export economics improve.
Lockheed makes PAC-3/THAAD missile defense systems and naval munitions; Gulf escalation drives FMS demand from GCC partners and US replenishment orders.
Raytheon makes Standard Missiles and Patriot interceptors used to defend Gulf assets; minesweeping/escort operations consume munitions inventory triggering reorders.

losers.rationale: Delta has highest jet fuel sensitivity among US legacies with limited hedging; Brent spike directly hits CASM and Q3 margins.
United heavy transpacific/international exposure means jet fuel + potential Gulf airspace rerouting both pressure unit costs.
Southwest's all-domestic 737 fleet has zero geographic hedge and historically light fuel hedge book — pure margin compression from crude spike.
Carnival bunker fuel costs spike with Brent; Middle East itineraries (Dubai/Abu Dhabi) at risk of cancellation. Consumer discretionary double-hit.
Dow's ethylene/petrochemical feedstock costs rise with crude; margin compression as price hikes lag input cost spike.
Walmart's freight and last-mile diesel costs rise; low-income consumer (core demo) hit hardest by gasoline price spike, pressuring discretionary basket.

headlines: Iran has mined 'large segments' of Hormuz Strait, Secretary of State Rubio says
